post.description:
Creates a new payment invoice in PREPARED status. The invoice is not yet active — use the [Activate Invoice](/api/invoice-activate) endpoint to assign a wallet and begin accepting payments.

**Typical flow**: Create Invoice → Activate Invoice → Monitor status via webhooks.

> **Note:** Set a `webhookUrl` to receive real-time status updates. Do not rely solely on polling — use webhooks as the primary notification mechanism.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Pagination cursor. Pass the `id` of the last item from the previous response to fetch the next page.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of items per page (1-100, default: 20)

parameters.name: sortDirection
parameters.description: Sort order: `asc` (oldest first) or `desc` (newest first, default)

get.description:
Retrieves a paginated list of invoices for the authenticated business account. Supports cursor-based pagination, filtering by status, and full-text search.

**Pagination**: Uses cursor-based pagination. Pass the `id` of the last item from the previous response as the `cursor` parameter to fetch the next page.

> **Tip:** Use the `statuses` filter to monitor invoices requiring attention (e.g., `ACTIVE`, `CONFIRMING`).

Retrieves a single invoice by its public ID. Returns the full invoice object including payment details, exchange rate, and current status.

> **Note:** Use this endpoint to verify invoice state before taking action. Always confirm the invoice status server-side rather than relying on client-side state.

patch.description:
Updates mutable fields on an existing invoice. Only invoices in `PREPARED` or `ACTIVE` status can be updated.

Updatable fields: `customerEmail`, `customerName`, `reference`, `redirectUrl`, `webhookUrl`, `expiredAt`.

> **Important:** Use [Activate Invoice](/api/invoice-activate) for payment configuration and [Cancel Invoice](/api/invoice-cancel) for cancellation — this endpoint only updates metadata fields.

400.description: Bad request - Invoice cannot be updated in current status

post.description:
Activates a `PREPARED` invoice by configuring its payment method. This assigns a blockchain wallet, fetches the current exchange rate, and transitions the invoice to `ACTIVE` status.

**Required:** `sourceCurrencyId` — the cryptocurrency the customer will pay with.
**Optional:** `expiredAt` — custom expiration timestamp.

> **Note:** The exchange rate is locked at activation time. Once activated, the invoice begins accepting payments immediately.

400.description: Bad request - Invoice cannot be activated or missing required fields
Bad request - Invoice cannot be cancelled in current status

post.description:
Cancels an invoice. Only invoices in `PREPARED` or `ACTIVE` status can be cancelled. Cancelled invoices cannot be reactivated.

> **Warning:** Cancelling an `ACTIVE` invoice will reject any incoming payments. Ensure no payment is in transit before cancelling.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of items per page (1-1000, default: 10)

parameters.name: sortDirection
parameters.description: Sort order: `asc` (oldest first) or `desc` (newest first, default)

parameters.name: currencyAlias
parameters.description: Filter balances by currency alias (e.g., usdt_binance)

parameters.name: currencyId
parameters.description: Deprecated. Use currencyAlias instead. Will be removed in a future version.

parameters.name: isParentOnly
parameters.description: Filter to only include parent currencies (default: true)

parameters.name: currencyType
parameters.description: Filter by currency type (FIAT, CRYPTO, NFT)

get.description:
Returns all account balances for the authenticated business. Each balance represents a currency holding with available and pending amounts.

Supports filtering by currency type (`FIAT`, `CRYPTO`), currency alias, and parent-only currencies.

> **Tip:** Use `isParentOnly=true` (default) to see aggregated balances. Set to `false` to see balances broken down by network.

403.description: Forbidden - Balance does not belong to business

get.description: Retrieves a specific account balance by its public ID. Returns the current balance amount, currency details, and pending transactions.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of items per page (1-1000, default: 10)

parameters.name: sortDirection
parameters.description: Sort order: `asc` (oldest first) or `desc` (newest first, default)

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Filter by history type (INVOICE, PAYOUT, PAYOUT_FEE, ADJUSTMENT)

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter by history status (CREDIT, DEBIT, NEGATIVE_ADJ)

403.description: Forbidden - Balance does not belong to business

get.description:
Returns the transaction history for a specific account balance. Each entry represents a credit or debit event (invoice payment, payout, fee, or manual adjustment).

Supports filtering by transaction type, status, date range, and currency.

> **Tip:** Use `type=INVOICE` to see incoming payments and `type=PAYOUT` to see outgoing transfers.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of items per page (1-1000, default: 10)

parameters.name: sortDirection
parameters.description: Sort order: `asc` (oldest first) or `desc` (newest first, default)

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Filter by history type (INVOICE, PAYOUT, PAYOUT_FEE, ADJUSTMENT)

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter by history status (CREDIT, DEBIT, NEGATIVE_ADJ)

403.description: Forbidden - Balance does not belong to business

get.description:
Downloads the transaction history for a specific account balance as a CSV file. Supports the same filters as the [Get Balance History](/api/account-balance-get-balance-history) endpoint.

> **Note:** The response is a CSV file download, not JSON. Use this for accounting, reconciliation, or reporting purposes.

Returns all supported crypto assets with their current exchange rates to fiat currencies and available blockchain networks.

Optionally filter by specific fiat currencies using the `currencies` parameter.

> **Note:** Exchange rates are indicative and may vary at the time of invoice activation. The final rate is locked when an invoice is activated.

parameters.name: currencies
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of fiat currency symbols to filter (e.g., USD,IDR)

401.description: Unauthorized - Missing or invalid API key

get.description:
Returns exchange rates for a specific crypto asset (e.g., BTC, ETH, USDT) to all supported fiat currencies, including available networks.

> **Tip:** Use this endpoint to display estimated prices to customers before creating an invoice.

parameters.name: asset
parameters.description: Crypto asset symbol (e.g., BTC, ETH, USDT)

parameters.name: currencies
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of fiat currency symbols to filter (e.g., USD,IDR)

401.description: Unauthorized - Missing or invalid API key

get.description:
Returns all supported cryptocurrencies and their details, including symbol, name, network, and contract address.

Use the `isActive` filter to exclude deprecated or disabled currencies. Filter by `networkId` to see currencies available on a specific blockchain.

> **Note:** The `id` returned here is the `sourceCurrencyId` required when activating an invoice.

400.description: Currency not available via this API

get.description:
Retrieves a specific currency by its alias (e.g., `usdt_binance`, `btc_bitcoin`). The alias uniquely identifies a currency on a specific network.

> **Tip:** Use this endpoint to look up currency details when you already know the alias from a previous API response.

parameters.name: blockchainType
parameters.description: Filter by blockchain type (EVM, TRON, SOLANA)

get.description:
Returns all supported blockchain networks with their details, including chain ID, blockchain type, and active status.

Filter by `blockchainType` to see only EVM-compatible chains, Solana, or Tron networks.
Retrieves a specific blockchain network by its slug (e.g., `arbitrum-one`, `ethereum`, `tron`).

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Pagination cursor. Pass the `id` of the last item from the previous response to fetch the next page.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of items per page (1-100, default: 20)

parameters.name: sortDirection
parameters.description: Sort order: `asc` (oldest first) or `desc` (newest first, default)

parameters.name: processingType
parameters.description: Filter by payout provider processing type - prioritize EXTERNAL for automated processing

get.description:
Retrieves a paginated list of payouts for the authenticated business. Supports cursor-based pagination, filtering by status, payout type, and processing type.

**Payout types:** `FIAT_FIAT`, `FIAT_CRYPTO`, `CRYPTO_FIAT`, `CRYPTO_CRYPTO`.
**Processing types:** `INTERNAL` (on-platform), `EXTERNAL` (blockchain/bank transfer).

> **Tip:** Filter by `processingType=EXTERNAL` to see payouts processed through external providers.

parameters.name: X-Idempotency-Key
parameters.description: Optional idempotency key to prevent duplicate payout creation. Use a unique string (e.g., UUID) to ensure the same request returns the same result if retried.

201.description: The business payout has been created successfully.

409.description: Conflict - Payout creation already in progress for this idempotency key

post.description:
Creates a new payout to transfer funds from your account balance to an external recipient.

**Required header:** `X-Idempotency-Key` — a unique string (e.g., UUID) to prevent duplicate payouts if the request is retried.

> **Warning:** Payouts are irreversible once processed. Always use the [Inquire Account](/api/payout-inquire-account) and [Get Payout Info](/api/payout-get-payout-info) endpoints to validate recipient details and preview fees before creating a payout.

> **Important:** The idempotency key ensures that retrying the same request (e.g., due to network timeout) will not create a duplicate payout.

200.description: The account details have been retrieved successfully.

post.description:
Validates and retrieves account details for a payout recipient. Use this before creating a payout to verify the recipient's account information and ensure the transfer will succeed.

> **Recommended flow:** Inquire Account → Get Payout Info → Create Payout.

200.description: The payout information has been retrieved successfully.

post.description:
Returns fee breakdown, validation requirements, and estimated delivery time for a payout. Use this to preview the total cost before committing to a payout.

> **Tip:** Show the fee breakdown to users before they confirm the payout to avoid surprises.

200.description: The payout status has been retrieved successfully.

get.description:
Returns the current status of a specific payout. Use this for polling-based status checks.

**Payout statuses:** `REQUESTED` → `PENDING` → `PROCESSING` → `CONFIRMING` → `COMPLETED` (or `FAILED`/`EXPIRED`).

> **Tip:** Prefer webhooks over polling for real-time status updates.

403.description: Forbidden - Business API access or ownership required

get.description: Retrieves the full details of a specific payout, including recipient information, fees, exchange rate, and transaction hash (if applicable).

info.title: Appreal API - Business
info.description: API used for on-ramp and off-ramp transaction

tags.name: Invoice
tags.description: Create, manage, and track payment invoices

tags.name: Balances
tags.description: View account balances and transaction history

tags.name: Currencies
tags.description: List supported currencies and their details

reference.description: Arbitrary reference string or object to attach to the invoice

redirectUrl.description: URL to redirect the customer after payment

nonce.description: Idempotency key to prevent duplicate invoices

webhookUrl.description: Webhook URL to receive payment status updates

currency.description: Currency symbol (e.g., ETH, USDT). Required if currencyId not provided.

network.description: Network name (e.g., ethereum, polygon). Required if currencyId not provided.

currencyId.description: Currency ID (UUID). Alternative to currency + network.

expiredAt.description: Custom expiration date/time (ISO 8601). If not provided, uses default.

sourceCurrencyAlias.description: Source currency alias for payment (e.g., usdt_binance). Preferred over sourceCurrencyId.

sourceCurrencyId.description: Source currency UUID. Use sourceCurrencyAlias when possible.

expiredAt.description: Custom expiration date/time (ISO 8601). If not provided, uses default.

amountInWei.description: Balance amount in Wei (for on-chain assets)

assets.description: List of all crypto assets with rates

validUntil.description: Timestamp when rates expire and should be refreshed
Timestamp when rates expire and should be refreshed

buyRate.description: Buy rate (what user pays to buy base currency)

sellRate.description: Sell rate (what user receives when selling base currency)

baseCurrencyInfo.description: Base currency info with name and logo

quoteCurrencyInfo.description: Quote currency info with name and logo

network.description: Network the currency is deployed on

accountId.description: Account ID that will be charged for this payout. Optional for merchants (uses their account), Account identifier

destinationCurrencyId.description: Target currency ID (what the recipient will receive)

sourceCurrencyId.description: Source currency ID (what will be deducted from account)

routingId.description: Payout Provider Routing ID for routing the payout

recipientId.description: Existing recipient ID from payout_fiat_recipients or payout_cryptocurrency_recipients table

recipientName.description: Recipient full name (required if recipientId not provided)

recipientIdNumber.description: Recipient ID number (required if recipientId not provided)

recipientPlaceOfBirth.description: Recipient place of birth (required if recipientId not provided)

recipientBirthDate.description: Recipient birth date (required if recipientId not provided)

recipientAddress.description: Recipient address (required if recipientId not provided)

recipientBankAccountNumber.description: Bank account number for bank transfers

description.description: Description or purpose of the payout

destinationCurrencyId.description: Target currency ID for the account inquiry

sourceCurrencyId.description: Source currency ID for the inquiry

routingId.description: Payout Provider Routing ID for routing the inquiry

amount.description: Amount to get payout info for

destinationCurrencyId.description: Target currency ID (what the recipient will receive)

sourceCurrencyId.description: Source currency ID (what will be deducted from account)

accountId.description: Account ID to get payout info for. Optional for merchants (uses their account), Account identifier
